title: Using a formula column that results in a date, that can be used in a gantt chart
excerpt: I created a Formula column. The formula I used is an IF statement that returns a date if true. The issue I’m having is that, when I go to the Gantt chart view, my Formula column does not appear in the choices of “Choose …

posts.author: davidcom (David Hill)
posts.cookedHtml: I created a Formula column. The formula I used is an IF statement that returns a date if true. The issue I’m having is that, when I go to the Gantt chart view, my Formula column does not appear in the choices of “Choose timeline columns”. Is there something I’m missing? Thanks!

posts.author: davidcom (David Hill)
posts.cookedHtml: I posted the original question a couple days ago. Is there a moderator that can answer?

posts.author: KMcG (Kelly McGarity)
posts.cookedHtml:
I could not agree more! I’m not sure why this function has not been added, nor do I understand how no moderator has replied regarding this potential upgrade, in almost a year.

It was a bit of a task to force that formula column to show as a date (which I figure would be easier if we had a ‘format column’ option), but now that it’s showing the date, it’s useless until I can use this data for a gantt chart.

posts.author: meganmansfield (Megan Mansfield)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb do you happen to have any insight on this topic? I am having the same issue.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is the biggest limitation of the formula column. You may want to browse the Feature request section of this community and add your voice to those asking for the ability to use formula columns in calendars, automations, etc. as well.

Currently, the only option available is to use a third-party app. I recommend checking out the Advanced Formula Booster. This tool bypasses the formula column and enables you to execute formulas that can update various types of columns, including a date column as per your requirement.

posts.author: meganmansfield (Megan Mansfield)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the app recommendation, I’ll check it out! It would definitely be a game changer if it works.

posts.author: MonkeyWithKeyboard (Devon)
posts.cookedHtml: For anyone in the future. There is a workflow option to Convert “Text to Date” or “Text to Number”. You can use this to convert the formula result into a usable item.

posts.author: JD182 (Jan Deichmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thx for the information. @Julietteb can you please tell me, when its fully avaiable for the Pro users or can I apply me for this, to get it earlier? We need this so badly.
